label: Guest content layer label, e.g. manuscript name, part, and guest content description
locus: Locus of the guest content layer, e.g. f. 1v or ff. 1v-4r
summary: A summary of the guest content layer. Describes what has been identified, how it was added, and its relationship to the main manuscript.
extent: Extent of the guest content, e.g. 4 ff. or f. 1v (partial)

writing.locus: Locus of the guest content script
note: A note about the guest content script, e.g. informal 13th-century minuscule hand.
A second note about the guest content script.

ink.locus: Locus of the guest content ink
note: A note about the guest content ink, e.g. main text in black; rubrics in red.

layout.locus: Locus of the guest content layout
layout.dim: Writing area dimensions of the guest content, e.g. 80 x 120 mm
note: A note about the guest content layout, e.g. written in available space on the flyleaf.

text_unit.label: Guest content text unit label
text_unit.locus: Locus of the guest content text unit

para.locus: Locus of the possession note in the guest layer
para.as_written: Possession note text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the possession note

assoc_name.value: Owner or institution name as a free-text value

note: A note about this possession note in the guest layer.
A second note — this para entry demonstrates a multi-value subtype array.

para.locus: Locus of a reader note in the guest layer
para.as_written: Reader note text as written in the original script
translation: English translation of the reader note
note: A note about this reader note in the guest layer.

assoc_date.value: Origin date of the guest content as a human-readable string, e.g. 13th c. CE
note: A note about the guest content date, e.g. paleographic dating.

assoc_name.value: Annotator or owner name as a free-text value

note: A note about the repository of the guest content.

note.value: This is a general note about the guest content layer. Guest content records describe additions, marginal notes, or content on reused material.
This is a condition note for the guest content layer, e.g. ink partially faded; text legible with difficulty.

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string for a reference work cited at the layer level.
bib.alt_shelf: [Alternate shelfmark from an older catalog, e.g. 652]
note: A note about this bibliographic entry

bib.citation: Full bibliographic citation string. Second bib entry at layer level to show repeatability.

related_mss.label: Label for related guest content layers in other manuscripts
note: A note about the relationship between these guest content layers.

mss.label: Label of a related manuscript with similar guest content

program.label: Description program name
program.description: Full description of the program under which this layer record was created.

cataloguer.message: Cataloguer message describing what was done in this edit.
Second cataloguer entry to show the field is repeatable.
